evidence: Google Maps business pin QTS Hillsboro Data Center on a specific building at published address 4951 NE Huffman St; satellite shows large DC campus; current pin was ~340m off. Not VERIFIED: Google shows adjacent HIL1 vs HIL2 - exact building of the two not certain.

Google lists 'MARA Granbury Data Center' (category Data center) at 2001 Mitchell Bend Hwy with operator site maragranbury.com. Identity strong; in-browser satellite failed to render so footprint not visually confirmed this session.
Google lists 'NTT PH1 Datacenter' at 10256 E Elliot Rd Mesa with NTT site services.global.ntt and a photo of the DC building. Identity strong; satellite not confirmed in-browser this session.
No clear Google business listing for 'True North Data Centers' at this VA location; nearest result was a different operator (NTT VA3, 21583 Megawatt Dr). Needs deeper research.

CyberLynk MKE1 (Franklin Data Center). Operator site and multiple listings confirm exact address 10125 S 52nd St, Franklin, WI 53132. This is a small carrier-neutral colo (~100+ cabinets, 3 buildouts, ~18k sqft) housed inside a multi-tenant flex/office-park building, not a purpose-built DC. Census geocode landed at the east edge of / road beside the large dark-and-gray-roofed flex building; imagery shows that building has rooftop HVAC units but no distinctive DC signature (no substation, no cooling plant, no fencing), consistent with an in-building colo. Point moved off the road onto the flex-building rooftop centroid. Building likely but only one soft signal and it is a shared building, hence CANDIDATE.

BVTNORXB Beaverton Central Office, operated by Ziply Fiber (CLLI code). PeeringDB fac/14266 (visited) confirms address 4155 SW Cedar Hills Blvd, Beaverton, OR 97005, colo offering. Location is dense downtown Beaverton near the Beaverton Central MAX station; geocode landed on a street intersection. The nearest substantial building NW of the point has a flat blue-gray roof with rooftop mechanical equipment and a fairly windowless look consistent with a telco central office; point placed there. Minor ambiguity: several similar low-rise buildings nearby and a Yelp listing associates the same address with the mixed-use 'Round at Beaverton Central' development, so the exact building cannot be fully isolated. One decent signal -> CANDIDATE.

833 Chestnut Street is a single named downtown Philadelphia office/carrier-hotel building (SunGard PHL-833; carriers incl. AT&T, Cogent, Comcast, Lumen, Verizon, Zayo; colo on the 11th floor). Address is unambiguous -> one specific building, no competing look-alike. In the 800 block of Chestnut (between 8th and 9th) odd numbers fall on the south side; the large building immediately south of Chestnut St at the geocoded point shows prominent rooftop mechanical penthouses/cooling equipment (a data-center signature). Geocode landed on the street centerline; point moved onto that building's rooftop. Identity + building + rooftop-cooling signal all agree.

DataBank ATL1, 760 West Peachtree St NW, Atlanta, in Georgia Tech's Technology Square. Multiple sources confirm ATL1 (~94-98k sqft, Georgia Tech anchor, Southern Crossroads node) is part of the CODA development, a 645k sqft mixed-use complex on the east side of West Peachtree. Geocode landed at the SW street corner of a large pale-roofed building on the east side of West Peachtree matching the CODA podium; point moved onto that building. CANDIDATE rather than VERIFIED because ATL1 is only a ~98k-sqft portion of the much larger interconnected CODA complex, so the exact sub-structure/podium wing housing the DC cannot be pinned with certainty from imagery.

Cogent Boca Raton, 5050 Conference Way N, Boca Raton, FL 33431. Confirmed this address = the Boca Raton Innovation Campus (BRIC), the former IBM Boca R&D campus (birthplace of the IBM PC), described as the largest office complex in Florida: three interconnected facilities on 123 acres. Cogent's suite is only ~10,011 sqft (and a separate Blackstone/T-Rex DC also sits at the same address). Imagery shows a sprawling contiguous mega-building complex with the distinctive circular plaza; geocode landed on Conference Way N between building wings. A ~10k-sqft colo suite inside this interconnected megastructure cannot be isolated to a specific building/wing, and multiple candidate sections front the address -> AMBIGUOUS; coordinates left null.

SFN IA-Waterloo is South Front Networks, a small carrier-neutral interconnect ('carrier motel'). Street address 206 W 13th St confirmed: census geocoder returned 42.488810,-92.330357 and OSM returned 42.488780,-92.330435, in agreement. Esri satellite centered on that point shows a residential / light-industrial neighborhood block (small houses, a cluttered salvage-type yard, trees) with no windowless data-center box, no rooftop cooling array, no substation, and no security fencing. No specific data-center building is identifiable, so no point can be placed. Confirmed address but no resolvable DC building.

Level(3)/Lumen Chicago 1 at 111 N Canal St, Suite 200, Chicago IL 60606, confirmed operational across baxtel, datacentermap and datacenters.com (55,229 sqft, HIPAA/SOC/PCI certified). The address building is the River Center / 'Gogo Building', a large 840,000 sqft full-block former Butler Brothers Warehouse on the west bank of the Chicago River. Esri satellite clearly shows this large distinct rectangular building immediately west of the river, east of the Union Station train shed. OSM building node 'River Center' = 41.884066,-87.639377 corroborates the census geocode; point placed on that rooftop centroid, on the building, no competing look-alike. (Data center is a colocation suite within this identified office/telecom building.)

Westelcom Networks Plattsburgh at 24 Margaret St, Plattsburgh NY 12901 is a small 200 kW / 40-ton in-building colocation (per datacentermap/inflect). Address confirmed: census geocoder 44.696500,-73.453386 and OSM 44.696420,-73.453289 agree, in downtown Plattsburgh. Esri satellite centered there shows a downtown streetscape (Margaret Street), a small pedestrian plaza, parking lots and mixed commercial buildings; the geocode falls on the street/plaza. No standalone data-center building and no DC signature (cooling units, substation, fencing) are visible - it is a small colo inside a downtown multi-tenant building. No specific building resolvable.

Lumen West Haven at 2 Horton Place, West Haven CT 06516 confirmed on PeeringDB (fac/13692), which lists coordinates 41.289812,-72.970174; OSM independently places 2 Horton Place at 41.289804,-72.970183 (same spot), so the address geolocation is corroborated. However Esri satellite at that point shows an open lot with greenhouse/nursery hoop structures and small nondescript low buildings near Boston Post Road (US-1), with the University of New Haven complex to the NW. No windowless data-center box, rooftop cooling array, substation, or fenced DC yard is visible, and several small structures are candidates - cannot identify the specific building. Point withheld.

Neutron colocation lists 422 W Riverside Ave, Suite 1401, Spokane WA 99201 (datacenters.com). Commercial listings (LoopNet/CommercialCafe) confirm 422 W Riverside = the US Bank Building, a 16-story historic (1910) office tower, 180,698 sqft. Suite 1401 = 14th floor, consistent with a 16-story tower. In Esri imagery the tower is identifiable at the address by its long SE-cast shadow (only high-rise on the block); I moved the point off the road/intersection where the Census geocode (47.658011,-117.418417) landed onto the tower rooftop centroid ~47.6583,-117.4191. CANDIDATE not VERIFIED because Neutron is an interior colo tenant inside a multi-tenant office tower, so there is no external data-center signature (no rooftop chillers/substation) visible to independently confirm the facility from imagery.

EdgeConneX POR02 (EDCPOR02) is a small (~3 MW) unmarked edge data center listed at '6397 NE Evergreen Pkwy, Bldg A, Hillsboro OR 97124' (datacenterHawk), but other sources give '6297 NE Evergreen Pkwy, Bldg A' and the Census geocoder only matched '6397 NW EVERGREEN PKWY' (45.5505,-122.9101) - a different quadrant/street. Esri imagery of the area (AmberGlen/Evergreen corporate park) shows numerous near-identical flat-roofed office/flex buildings with no distinct data-center signature (no unmistakable rooftop cooling array, substation, or fencing) to single one out. Conflicting street numbers (6397 vs 6297) and quadrant (NE vs NW) plus multiple look-alike candidate buildings make building-level assignment impossible without guessing. Coords left null.

375 Pearl Street, New York NY 10038 is Sabey Data Centers' Intergate.Manhattan (SDC Manhattan), a purpose-built data center in the former Verizon/NYNEX tower - an iconic, unambiguous single building. Census geocode: 40.71086,-74.00069. Esri imagery clearly shows the distinctive tower immediately west of that point with extensive rooftop mechanical/cooling arrays (multiple large condenser/HVAC grids on the roof - a strong data-center signal), sitting directly against the Brooklyn Bridge/FDR approach ramps, with the X-shaped Alfred E. Smith Houses to the east confirming the location. Point moved onto the tower rooftop centroid ~40.7109,-74.0012. No competing look-alike; identity and building both certain.

Crown Castle Providence (RI1), 235 Promenade St, Providence RI 02908 - a Crown Castle Fiber (former Lightower) interconnection/colo facility. Census geocode (41.82855,-71.41991) lands on the Woonasquatucket River / Promenade St roadway. The 235 Promenade address corresponds to a large multi-building mixed-use complex (the Foundry district) on the river's north bank, comprising several adjoining brick mill and modern buildings. Esri imagery shows no single structure with an unmistakable data-center rooftop signature to isolate the colo, and the complex shares the address across multiple buildings, so the specific building cannot be determined without guessing. Coords left null.

QTS Phoenix (PHX2) is the QTS Phoenix II campus: DC1 at 1200 N 40th St plus DC2 (1250), DC3 (1400), DC4 (1450), DC5 (1500) N 40th St, Phoenix AZ 85008 - an ~80-acre, 5-building campus; DC1 was purpose-built and completed in 2023 (datacenterHawk). Census geocode 33.46233,-111.99513. Available Esri World Imagery of the address predates construction: it shows only a large bare dirt field and adjacent single-family residential housing at 40th Street, with no data-center buildings present. With no building visible in current imagery to place a rooftop point on (and the record being campus-level across 5 buildings), the exact building cannot be verified from imagery. Coords left null.

Colocation America 'HDC1' colo occupies 5th floor of the downtown office building at 108 Bank St, Waterbury CT (30,000 sqft facility). Census geocode lands squarely on the addressed multi-story building at the Bank St block in downtown Waterbury. In-building colo in a shared office tower so no exclusive DC signals visible from roof.

Consolidated Communications (former FairPoint/Verizon NET) telco facility at 266 Main St, downtown Burlington VT. Census geocode lands at the Main St / S Winooski Ave intersection; the block contains several mixed-use/institutional buildings and no windowless CO with clear DC signals can be distinguished from imagery. Multiple candidate buildings -> ambiguous.

Cogent Communications operates a very small colo (1,201 sqft, 777 sqft raised floor) at 3025 Hesper Rd, Billings MT. Satellite shows an industrial/yard area; census geocode lands on open ground near a road junction with several candidate industrial buildings (white metal building to SW, small structures). Tiny suite-level colo cannot be pinned to one building from imagery.

Sungard AS DEN-550 / now 365 Data Centers, 550 E 84th Ave Thornton CO, described as a 478,000 SF retrofit facility. Satellite SW of the geocoded 84th Ave intersection shows a single very large flat-roofed building surrounded by extensive parking, consistent with the large retrofit footprint. Point moved off the road onto that building centroid. Rooftop DC signals not distinct (retrofit) so CANDIDATE.

DataBank PHX1 (former zColo/Zayo) is a 16,200 sqft in-building colo, suite D151, at 3110 N Central Ave, downtown Phoenix. Census geocode lands on N Central Ave at the addressed office building. Shared multi-tenant tower, correct building identified but no exclusive DC signals -> CANDIDATE.

EdgeConneX EDCMAD01 is a small 7,800 sqft colo at 4916 E Broadway, Madison WI. Census geocode failed onto the US-12/18 Beltline freeway; the addressed commercial strip sits just north. Several similar commercial/office buildings line the frontage and the small colo cannot be tied to one building or shown with DC signals -> ambiguous. Coords moved onto the most prominent frontage building as best estimate.

Telehouse Los Angeles occupies the 626 Wilshire Blvd carrier hotel (156,000 sqft building, 13,000 sqft raised floor, est. 1998). Census geocode lands on the 626 Wilshire building in downtown LA's telecom district. In-building colo in a shared carrier hotel -> CANDIDATE per correct-building-identified.

Digital Realty LAX10/LOS1 at 600 W 7th St LA - the former JW Robinson's department store, a 7-story, 490,000 sqft full-block building, second-best-connected building in LA after One Wilshire. Census geocode lands on that distinctive full-block building. Multi-tenant carrier hotel -> CANDIDATE.

iM Critical Miami (former AT&T/BellSouth wire center, 'Miami Data Vault'), 100 NE 80th Terrace - a purpose-built 100,000 sqft single-operator data center, Cat-5 hardened. Satellite shows a distinct light-roofed windowless box with rooftop mechanical units and a secured/fenced compound matching a former telco wire center, standing out from the surrounding residential/retail fabric with no competing look-alike. Point placed on that building's rooftop centroid.

CoreSite LA4 (now Verizon) at 800 S Hope St LA - the 8-story, 242,000 sqft 'Bullocks Building', a downtown carrier hotel. Census geocode lands on the large distinctive full-block flat-roofed building at center of image. Multi-tenant in-building colo, correct building identified -> CANDIDATE.

BendTel Inc (Central Oregon ISP) operates a small colo/interconnect facility at 130 NW Greenwood Ave, downtown Bend OR (~2MW). Census geocode lands on the addressed downtown Bend commercial building. Small in-building/telco colo, correct building identified, no exclusive DC signals -> CANDIDATE.

Netrality's 1301 Fannin, Houston - a 25-story, ~1M sqft carrier hotel (built 1984 as the Texas banking data center / Federal Reserve vault), Houston's primary interconnection hub with 40+ carriers. Census geocode lands on the tower footprint in downtown Houston. Purpose-built enterprise DC/carrier-hotel tower, correct building identified; multi-tenant so CANDIDATE.

Quad State Internet operates the METRO1 carrier-neutral colocation (remote-hands, cross-connects) published at 506 Market St, Metropolis IL. Census geocode places 506 Market St squarely on a downtown commercial block. Esri imagery shows only ordinary small-town commercial buildings with no distinct rooftop cooling/DC signature - this is an in-building colo occupying a suite in an existing building, so the address is precise but the exact building cannot be visually distinguished from its neighbors. Point left on the geocoded address.

Given coordinate (no street address in source) resolves to Google's Loudoun County VA campus. Esri imagery shows an unmistakable hyperscale data center: a very large windowless white-roof building with long parallel rows of rooftop cooling/air-handling units and an adjacent electrical substation/switchyard to the north. Geocode landed on the road between two look-alike DC buildings; point moved onto the centroid of the SW building. Marked CANDIDATE rather than VERIFIED because two adjacent identical DC buildings on the same campus create a which-building ambiguity for the exact Google facility.

Lenoir City Utilities Board houses a Tier III, FEMA hazard-resistant data center with redundant on-site substations inside its ~180,000 sqft operations HQ at 7698 Creekwood Park Blvd. The Census geocode of that address landed NW on the US-321 interchange with no building, but a wider Esri view revealed the large multi-wing LCUB HQ complex ~350m SE with a clearly adjacent electrical switchyard - matching the record's original stored coords. Point moved onto that building's rooftop centroid. It is the only large facility in the area with no competing look-alike, and the substation is a strong DC signal.

50 Orin Way is a remote high-desert parcel outside Douglas WY. Census geocoded the address, and Esri imagery at that point shows only a small rural compound (a white metal building, a red-roofed structure, and a few scattered vehicles/containers on dirt tracks) with NO data-center signature: no container-yard rows, no cooling plant, no scaled substation, no security-fenced box. No source I found confirms that operator 'ACT' runs a data center at this exact address (results only cover ACT's Cheyenne site and general WY crypto activity). Cannot confirm a DC building, so coords left null.

Equinix DC8 is a small (24,576 sqft) single-story colo at 8502A Tyco Rd in Tysons/Vienna VA. Census matched 8502 Tyco Rd but returned zip 22182 vs the record's 22180. Esri imagery at the geocode shows a large graded/cleared parcel (apparent redevelopment/construction) occupying the center of the block, surrounded by dense commercial buildings including what looks like an auto dealership/retail. The specific single-story DC8 building could not be confidently distinguished from the surrounding redevelopment, so no building point placed.

QuadraNet's downtown LA HQ is an in-building colocation (60,000+ sqft over 7 suites) inside the 'LA Telecom Center' carrier hotel at 530 W 6th St, adjacent to One Wilshire, sharing the building with Level 3, AT&T, Cogent and Windstream. Census geocode of 530 W 6th St lands on that building in dense downtown LA; zoomed imagery shows a substantial flat-roof building with a rooftop mechanical penthouse. Correct carrier-hotel tower is identified (CANDIDATE per in-building-colo rule); exact floors not distinguishable from imagery.

Lumen (ex-Level 3/CenturyLink) operates a colocation/telecom facility at 54 Meadow St, New Haven CT. Census geocode lands on a cluster of substantial flat-roofed buildings just west of the Metro-North rail yards and I-95, consistent with a telecom central office. Several similar large buildings sit at the geocode so the exact one is not definitively distinguishable from imagery; in-building telecom colo with a precise published address. Point left on the geocoded address.

DataBank's official IAD2 datasheet lists the McLean facility at 1764A Old Meadow Lane, McLean VA 22102 - the record's '1674A Old Meadow Lane' is a digit-transposition typo (the record's own stored coords 38.9198,-77.2156 sit beside 1764 Old Meadow Ln, not the erroneous 1674 geocode which lands 500m NE on Old Meadow Rd). Corrected Census geocode of 1764 Old Meadow Ln places it in a Tysons/McLean office park. In-building colo; exact office building among several similar mid-rise office buildings is not visually distinguishable, but the corrected address is well-sourced. Point set to corrected geocode.

T5@Cleveland is a 187,000 sqft, 26 MW data center at 4150 W 150th St, Cleveland OH. Census geocode places the address on the west side of W 150th St; zoomed Esri imagery shows a single large multi-section flat-roofed building with visible rooftop mechanical/cooling units on that side, matching the address. Point moved onto the building's centroid. Marked CANDIDATE (not VERIFIED) because several large industrial/warehouse look-alike buildings sit immediately east across W 150th St.

QTS Piscataway PNJ1 (formerly Dupont Fabros NJ1), 101 Possumtown Rd, a 360,000 sqft standalone data center. Esri satellite at the census geocode (40.5569,-74.4888) shows one large windowless industrial building with a distinctive large white membrane roof section, isolated in parkland and fronting Possumtown Rd, with an adjacent equipment/parking yard. No competing look-alike. Point moved from the road-frontage geocode north onto the rooftop centroid.

ACT (Advanced Communications Technology) Riverton, a small rural telco POP at 115 S 7th E. Census geocoded exactly to 115 S 7TH ST E. Esri image shows a small-town commercial downtown grid with modest single-story buildings and no distinctive data-center signature (no visible rooftop cooling array, substation, or fencing) to isolate the exact unit. Point left at the geocoded street address; identity confirmed but building not visually distinguishable.

ColoCrossing LA1 is an in-building colocation at 530 W 6th Street, Suite 901 (datacenterHawk operator listing), in the downtown LA financial district near One Wilshire. Census geocoded to 530 W 6TH ST. Esri image shows dense downtown high-rises; the correct shared office tower is identified by street address but the data center occupies a single suite (9th floor), so no independent rooftop signature. Point on the geocoded building.

TSR Solutions occupies Suite 825 (8th floor) at 324 E Wisconsin Ave, Milwaukee. Nominatim resolves 324 E Wisconsin Ave to the named 'Wells Building' (43.0389885,-87.9069379), a specific downtown high-rise. Esri image shows dense downtown blocks; the correct building is pinpointed by the named-POI match but the facility is an in-building suite with no independent rooftop signal. Point on the Wells Building.

Equinix DE1 (former SwitchData), a small 18,900 sqft single-story facility at 9706 E Easter Ave, Suite 160, Englewood. Census geocoded exactly to 9706 E EASTER AVE. Esri image shows a business/flex park; a low building near the geocode center carries visible rooftop mechanical units consistent with a small colo, but it is one of several similar flex buildings. Point placed on that building.

Fiber Alley at 8917 Complex Dr, San Diego (Kearny Mesa). Census geocoded exactly to 8917 COMPLEX DR. Esri image shows a dense cluster of near-identical white flat-roofed flex/industrial buildings; no distinctive data-center signature (cooling array, substation, container yard) is visible to isolate the exact unit from its look-alikes. Point left at the geocoded street address.

ACT Cheyenne, a small telco POP listed at 1621 Bruns Way. The address does not geocode in the Census or Nominatim geocoders, and no 'Bruns Way' could be located in OSM or web sources. The dataset's stored coordinates (41.1310,-104.9009) land in open prairie at a dirt-road junction with only a small shed visible in Esri imagery; no identifiable data-center building can be confirmed. Coordinates left null.

123.NET DC2 at 24275 Northwestern Hwy, Southfield MI. Census geocoded exactly to 24275 NORTHWESTERN HWY. Esri image shows a single standalone building at the geocode, fronting Northwestern Hwy, with a distinct cluster of white rooftop cooling/mechanical units - a strong data-center signal - and adjacent equipment yard. No competing building at the address. Point on the rooftop centroid.

LightEdge Kansas City KCI2 is the Lenexa/Meritex 'Cavern' facility (acquired from Cavern Technologies), located 125 ft underground inside a limestone formation; the dataset address 'Pillar #18-33' confirms an underground pillar location. There is no rooftop/building signature visible from satellite - Esri imagery over the surface (38.9519,-94.7896) shows only surface warehouse structures unrelated to the underground data hall. No above-ground building to point to; coordinates left null.

Metanet at 7 Teleport Drive, Staten Island - part of 'The Teleport' corporate/telecom park. Census geocoded to 7 TELEPORT DR. Esri image shows one large isolated building with a light roof and rooftop mechanical equipment set on a big campus lot, separated from surrounding residential streets. The geocode landed on the access road/lot, so point moved onto that building. Multi-building park so exact tenant building carries some ambiguity.

TierPoint Baltimore BWI, a 35,000 sqft facility at 813 Pinnacle Drive, Linthicum Heights. Census returned no match, but Nominatim has a named POI 'TierPoint Baltimore BWI, 813 Pinnacle Drive, BWI Tech Park' at 39.21345,-76.68286. Esri image at those coords shows a cluster of large flat-roofed flex buildings; the POI-indicated building at center carries rooftop mechanical units. Business park with look-alike buildings, so medium confidence. Point on the POI building.

DataBank Cleveland CLE1 is a downtown in-building colocation at 1225 Euclid Avenue, Cleveland. Census geocoded exactly to 1225 EUCLID AVE. Esri image shows dense downtown high-rise blocks; the correct building is pinpointed by street address but the facility occupies floors within a shared downtown building, so no independent rooftop data-center signature. Point on the geocoded building.

Coloblox ATL2 at 55 Marietta St NW, Atlanta. Baxtel/datacentermap/inflect confirm both Coloblox ATL1 and ATL2 (1.2MW, chilled-water cooling) are colo suites inside the single '55 Marietta Street' carrier hotel building, so the building is unambiguous by address. Census geocode 33.755474,-84.391094 lands on that downtown block. In-building colo with no external DC signature (rooftop cooling/substation not visible from imagery) in a dense high-rise financial district, so building assignment rests on the address alone -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the geocoded building.

EdgeCore Silicon Valley (SV01), 2201 Laurelwood Rd, Santa Clara - a purpose-built 2-building/540k-sqft campus that broke ground 2023 and launched first facility 2025 (edgecore.com, DCD). Census geocode fell on the Hwy-237/Great America interchange (wrong); at the dataset coord Esri imagery of the address shows a large building mid-construction with a gridded/mechanical roof (center-bottom) plus other large flat-roof buildings nearby. The construction-state building matches the new SV01 build and I placed the point on it, but imagery predates completion and there are competing large buildings in this dense Santa Clara DC cluster, so exact building not certain -> CANDIDATE.

QTS Manassas (MAN1), 9340 Godwin Drive, Manassas VA (datacentermap/PeeringDB fac/7874). Census geocode did not match; using the address area, Esri imagery unmistakably shows a large purpose-built QTS data-center campus: two very large white windowless DC boxes with regular rooftop patterns, an adjacent large electrical substation/transformer yard (center), generator/cooling yards and blue container/generator arrays to the NE - all strong DC signatures. Because it is a multi-building campus (DC1/DC2/DC3) I cannot isolate which box is specifically MAN1/DC1; point moved off the central substation onto the large SE DC building -> CANDIDATE.

Router12 Networks (a local Mason City ISP) hosts colo inside the Brick and Tile Building, a historic 8-story steel-framed multi-tenant office building at 103 E State St, Mason City IA (router12.net; Yelp/leasing listings confirm the building). Census geocode 43.151758,-93.199317 lands in the small downtown grid on that block. This is a small in-building colo with no external DC signature (no cooling array/substation/fencing); the building is identifiable only by address, so building-level accuracy rests on the geocode -> CANDIDATE.

Equinix LA2 at 818 W 7th Street, 6th Floor, Los Angeles - an 8-story reinforced-concrete multi-tenant telecom-hotel building (datacentermap/cloudandcolocation/PeeringDB fac/102). The colo is an interior 6th-floor suite (~33k sqft), so no external DC signature is visible. Census geocode 34.049020,-118.259396 lands on the 818 W 7th building in the downtown LA financial district; the specific building is fixed by the unique street address but cannot be independently confirmed from imagery signature -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the geocoded building.

Digital Realty DFW16 at 1232 Alma Rd, Richardson TX - a single-story ~105,800 sqft data center on Digital Realty's 69-acre 'Datacenter Park - Dallas' campus, notable for its own on-site private substation (100+ MW) (digitalrealty.com/dfw16). Census geocode 32.963480,-96.717178 landed on Alma Rd; Esri imagery just NE shows a large single-story building with an extensive rooftop condenser/cooling array (clear DC signature) consistent with DFW16. Point moved off the road onto that building. CANDIDATE (not VERIFIED) because it is one building within a multi-building Digital Realty campus of similar structures.

Argo Data Center = the 'Helios' facility in Dickens County TX (near Afton/McAdoo), Argo Blockchain's flagship ~125,000 sqft / 180MW bitcoin-mining center, since sold to Galaxy and being converted for CoreWeave (TAMU TRERC; EverythingLubbock). Dataset coord 33.777264,-100.869 is bare graded dirt; re-centering ~300m south, Esri imagery shows a long windowless white mining/DC building with rooftop labeling, an adjacent large substation/transformer yard (strong power signature) and crypto-container/equipment rows amid heavy construction. Point placed on that main building. CANDIDATE rather than VERIFIED because the site has expanded to a sprawling 1,500-acre multi-structure campus, so the exact original building cannot be cleanly isolated.

EvoSwitch WDC1 at 9651 Hornbaker Road, Manassas VA - a 235,000 sqft purpose-built DC (leased in the CloudHQ Manassas Hornbaker building) (datacentermap/baxtel). Census geocode 38.746361,-77.533563 landed on a road/field; Esri imagery to the NE (matching the dataset's own suggested coord) shows a large gray flat-roof data-center building with a rooftop cooling-unit array along its east side and a landscaped parking entrance. Point placed on that building. CANDIDATE because the immediate area is a CloudHQ campus with several near-identical large DC buildings, so a minor which-building ambiguity remains.

UnitedLayer Los Angeles, 530 W 6th Street, LA - the operator's own release describes it as colo inside the '530 West 6th Street datacenter and office park', a premier LA carrier hotel (unitedlayer.com; datacenterHawk). It is an interior colo (4.2MW, N+1 CRAC) with no external DC signature. Census geocode 34.048222,-118.254575 lands on the 530 W 6th building in the downtown LA financial district; the building is fixed by the unique address but not independently confirmable from imagery -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the geocoded building.

Centersquare LAX4 (formerly Cyxtera/Evoque LA4) at 2301 W 120th Street, Hawthorne CA - a large ~288,583 sqft colocation facility (~28MW) (baxtel/datacenters.com; PeeringDB fac/6669). Census geocode 33.923770,-118.320589 lands at the site just south of the I-105 freeway; Esri imagery there shows a large windowless flat-roof building complex with some rooftop mechanical, matching a ~288k-sqft DC footprint. Point kept on the geocoded campus building. Correct as a colo-in-building CANDIDATE.

CyrusOne Allen (DFW campus), 840 Allen Commerce Pkwy. Census matched the address; satellite shows a multi-building CyrusOne data center campus: a large windowless upper building with a full row of rooftop/side cooling+generator units, and a second large box (rooftop solar/cooling, generators) to the SW. Geocode falls between them; point placed on the nearest (upper) large DC building. Minor ambiguity over which campus building is the exact street number, hence CANDIDATE.

Amplex Internet HQ (small NW-Ohio WISP/ISP offering colo), 22690 Pemberville Rd, Luckey OH. Census matched the address to a rural crossroads. Satellite shows mostly farm fields; the only substantial structure at the corner is a gray metal building with a paved yard at the NE corner (point placed there). No data-center signature (no cooling farm, generators, or substation) is visible - consistent with a small ISP server room rather than a purpose-built DC. Low confidence that this specific building is Amplex vs a neighboring rural business.

QX.Net Lexington (now EarthLink Business), 333 W Vine St - an in-building colo (~2.1 MW) inside the Vine Center Tower in downtown Lexington. Census matched the exact address, landing on the downtown high-rise. In-building colo so no external DC signature; point kept on the geocoded tower.

WOW! Business Tampa colo, 400 N Tampa St, Suite 1000 - a ~10,000 sqft carrier-neutral colo inside Park Tower, a 36-story downtown Tampa high-rise. Census matched the exact address in the downtown core. In-building colo; point kept on the geocoded Park Tower building.

TierPoint Waterbury (WAT), 108 Bank St, 5th Floor - an in-building colo (~30,000 sqft, 1,600 sqft raised floor) in a downtown Waterbury office building. Census matched the exact address in the downtown grid. In-building/upper-floor colo so no rooftop DC signature; point kept on the geocoded building.

Alpheus Fort Worth (now LOGIX), 307 W 7th St - a carrier-neutral colo (~29,472 sqft footprint) in downtown Fort Worth. Census matched the exact address in the dense downtown core (near Bass Performance Hall). In-building colo; point kept on the geocoded building.

Equinix BO2 (former Verizon BOS1), 41-43 Alexander Rd, Billerica MA - a 108,000 sqft standalone data center. Census matched the address; satellite ~140m south of the geocode shows a large dark-roofed windowless building with a big adjacent solar array to the east and industrial/warehouse neighbors - consistent with the DC. Point placed on that building. Building's angular footprint is atypical so held at CANDIDATE rather than VERIFIED.

Centersquare/Csquare LAX5-A Irvine, 2681 Kelvin Ave - a 150,000 sqft standalone data center (15 MW, onsite fuel cell). Census matched the exact address; zoomed satellite shows a classic DC: large windowless white box with rooftop mechanical units and, at the NW corner, a fenced yard containing a row of ~10-12 cooling/generator units. Point placed on the building rooftop centroid.

DataBank AUS1, an 11,000 sqft in-building colo (Suite 300). ADDRESS DISCREPANCY: our record says 7128 McNeil Dr but multiple independent listings (datacenterHawk, inflect, datacentermap) give 7218 McNeil Dr - a different building ~180m SW. Census geocoded 7218 to a NW-Austin flex/office park with several near-identical white-roofed multi-tenant buildings; the exact suite building cannot be distinguished from lookalikes and there is no external DC signature. Point placed on the flex-park complex at the corrected 7218 address; low confidence due to address conflict and building ambiguity.

Cologix DSM2 (former OneNeck), 1205 Technology Parkway, Cedar Falls IA - a ~12,500 sqft colo (1 MW). Census matched the address to a suburban office/tech park (Cedar Valley TechWorks area). Satellite shows several standard office buildings with parking and no distinct data-center signature (no cooling farm, generators, or windowless box), consistent with a colo suite inside an office building. Point placed on the office-building cluster just north of the geocoded point; low confidence on the exact building.

Identity confirmed: BlueBridge Networks Mayfield Heights in-building colocation at 5915 Landerbrook Dr (30,074 sqft footprint, 150 kW N+1). Census geocode matched 5915 Landerbrook Dr. Imagery shows a suburban office park with several similar mid-size office buildings and no distinctive data-center signature (no rooftop chillers/substation) because it is a small suite inside a multi-tenant office building. Point placed on the office building nearest the geocoded address; exact building among the office cluster is not fully certain.

Identity confirmed: Centersquare IAD1 campus at 21110 Ridgetop Circle, Sterling VA, explicitly described as three single-story purpose-built fiber-linked buildings sharing one address. Imagery clearly shows multiple classic data-center buildings (bright white windowless boxes with rows of rooftop cooling units) in the Dulles corridor. Because IAD1-C, D and E share the single 21110 Ridgetop Circle address with no visual way to tell which building is IAD1-C/E, this is genuinely ambiguous. Coords given are a representative point on the nearest large DC building to the geocode.

Identity confirmed: Lumen/Level(3) Nashville 3 at 2208 9th Ave N, a small 40,000 sqft telco colo (5,719 sqft raised floor). Census geocode matched the exact street address. A distinct white-roofed single building sits at the geocoded location and coincides with the dataset's own suggested_lat/lon (36.18879,-86.803554). Small telco building identified and point placed on its roof centroid; low DC-signature but consistent single-building match.

Identity confirmed: Flexential Portland/Hillsboro 3 (PDX03) at 5419 NE Starr Blvd, 358,000 sqft, 36 MW purpose-built DC. Census geocode matched exact address. Imagery shows an unmistakable large windowless white building with a full row of rooftop cooling/CRAH units along its north edge at the geocoded point, in Hillsboro's data-center cluster. Point placed on that building's rooftop centroid.

Identity confirmed: Dakota Carrier Networks (DCN) Great Plains data center at 3901 Great Plains Dr S, Fargo. Census geocode matched exact street. Geocode landed on an adjacent open field; imagery shows a single distinctive low, purpose-designed building with parking immediately SW of the point (the DCN facility). Point moved onto that building. No competing look-alike but no visible rooftop-chiller confirmation, hence candidate.

Identity confirmed: COMSAT (now Goonhilly) Santa Paula satellite teleport / ground station at 7000 Pine Grove Rd on a 10-acre mountain site, published coords 34.413094,-119.080887. Census geocoder returned no match (rural). Esri imagery at the published coords shows a cluster of white antenna/dish structures in a mowed hilltop clearing east of center with an ops building nearby, matching a teleport. Point placed on the antenna field. Facility is a ground station rather than a windowless DC box, hence candidate not verified.

Identity confirmed: LightEdge Kansas City / SubTropolis (KCI1) at 9050 NE Underground Drive, Pillar 312 - a Tier III data center located 125 feet underground inside the SubTropolis limestone mine. Imagery at the geocoded address shows only the mine portals cut into the hillside and surface truck-terminal/warehouse buildings; the data center itself is subterranean with no rooftop or surface building to place a building-exact point on. Unresolvable by satellite imagery.

Identity confirmed: Flexential Dallas/Richardson (RCH01, former ViaWest) at 3010 Waterview Pkwy, 100,807 sqft, 3.75 MW. Census geocode matched the street but the interpolated point falls on an open field/running-track and campus area (UT Dallas / Telecom Corridor) with no identifiable windowless DC building at or adjacent to the point. Surrounding office buildings cannot be confidently distinguished as the Flexential building from imagery. Left coords null rather than guess.

Identity confirmed: Equinix DE2 (former Verizon DEN1) at 335 Inverness Drive South, Englewood - a one-story, 108,336 sqft slab-on-grade IBX. Census geocode matched exact address in the Inverness office park. Imagery shows a large low single-story building with an extensive parking lot immediately at/east of the geocoded point, consistent with a one-story telecom/DC building. Point placed on that building; office-park context with similar low buildings keeps it candidate not verified.

Identity confirmed: CyrusOne DUR1 / NC-1 at 2223 NE Creek Parkway, Durham (RTP), ~420,000 sqft across 11 data halls, 50 MW. Census geocode matched exact address; point landed in woods beside a large white purpose-built building complex to the NE, the dominant structure in the area (matching a 420k sqft facility) with rooftop equipment visible. Point moved onto that complex. Several other large white warehouse/industrial buildings nearby are competing look-alikes, so candidate.

Identity confirmed: Crown Castle Fiber CT1 / Hartford (960 Main) - an in-building colo (11,937 sqft footprint, former Lightower) inside a downtown Hartford office building at 960 Main St. Census geocode matched exact address. Imagery is dense downtown high-rises; the colo occupies a specific building at 960 Main but the exact tower cannot be confidently isolated from rooftop imagery. Point placed at the geocoded 960 Main St location; low confidence on exact building footprint.

Identity confirmed: T5@Silicon Valley at 39800 Eureka Dr, Newark, 133,200 sqft purpose-built DC (originally built by MCI WorldCom, later Apple, acquired by T5 in 2020) within the Pacific Research Center campus. Census geocode matched exact address. Imagery shows a large low building with visible rooftop mechanical/cooling equipment near the geocoded point; point placed on it. Campus contains several visually similar former-Apple buildings, so candidate rather than verified.

CoreSite VA2 (part of the VA1/VA2/VA3 Reston campus) at 12098 Sunrise Valley Drive, ~190k sqft colo. Census geocoder exact-matched the address (38.94895,-77.36449). Satellite shows a dense suburban office/mixed park (adjacent buildings are clearly offices with octagonal atriums, plus retail); the specific nondescript colo building could not be visually distinguished from neighboring offices, and the geocode point sits on the frontage road. Point left at the geocoded address as best reference.

200 Paul Avenue, San Francisco carrier hotel / data center. Census geocoder exact-matched the address. Satellite shows a multi-story windowless building topped with a dense array of rooftop mechanical/cooling units (rows of chillers) characteristic of a major data center, distinct from surrounding warehouses and residential blocks. Point placed on that building's rooftop centroid.

8425 North 114th Street is Google's Northwest Omaha hyperscale campus. Address did not geocode, so used dataset coordinate; that point landed on empty graded/construction land. Satellite clearly shows one large completed windowless data-center building with rows of rooftop cooling units at the bottom-right, with adjacent active construction. Moved the point off the empty land onto the completed building. CANDIDATE because it is a multi-building hyperscale campus (specific-building attribution).

Subrigo LAX11 is an in-building colocation suite (650 S Grand Ave, Suite 701) inside a downtown Los Angeles high-rise, not a standalone data-center building. Census geocoder exact-matched the address to the specific tower. Satellite is dense downtown high-rise grid; no distinct DC signature visible from the roof (expected for a suite-level colo). Point placed on the geocoded building footprint.

Centersquare/Csquare (former Evoque/Cyxtera) ATL1 campus at 375 Riverside Parkway, Lithia Springs (~86k sqft, <1mi from Google). Census geocoder exact-matched the address; point landed on the road. Satellite shows a large windowless building with a line of rooftop cooling units along its edge and a secured lot on the east side of Riverside Pkwy. Point moved from the road onto that building.

EdgeConneX Pittsburgh EDCPIT01 at 282 Corliss St, a 14,700 sqft retrofit of an existing building in the Corliss Street industrial corridor. Census geocoder exact-matched the address; point landed near the road/trees. Satellite shows a large flat-roofed industrial building on the parcel; being a small retrofit inside a larger industrial structure, the exact tenant footprint is one-signal. Point placed on the industrial building.

CyrusOne DFW1 at 1649 W Frankford Rd, Carrollton (large ~600-670k sqft campus). Census geocoder exact-matched the address. Satellite shows a business park with several large flat-roofed buildings; the large windowless building with scattered rooftop mechanical units nearest the geocoded point is the likely DC structure, but multiple similar large buildings create minor ambiguity on the exact building. Point placed on that building.

Long Lines Secure Data Center at 4647 Stone Ave, Sioux City is a small (~3,972 sqft) facility built into a hillside on the Western Iowa Tech Community College campus. Census geocoder exact-matched the address; point landed at the Stone Ave intersection. Because the facility is a small hillside/bunker structure, it is not distinctly visible as a standalone building in imagery. Point kept at the geocoded address.

ACT Gillette, 845 Southern Drive East, Gillette WY. Address did not geocode. Dataset coordinate (44.2468,-105.5047) lands on open prairie/agricultural fields astride a highway with no structures visible in satellite imagery. Web search on Campbell County crypto mines did not confirm a building at this address (likely a planned or unbuilt crypto/mining site). No building found; coords left null.

Opus Interactive HIO01 at 8135 Evergreen Pkwy, Hillsboro (~345k sqft, 24MW). Listings say NE Evergreen; census normalizes the address to NW Evergreen at this coordinate. Satellite (zoomed) shows an unambiguous data center: a large windowless box with a bank of rooftop cooling/generator units along the north edge, a large adjacent fenced electrical substation/switchyard, and perimeter security fencing. Point placed on the building.

Meta Hyperion hyperscale campus, Richland Parish, Louisiana. Dataset coordinate (32.4771,-91.6323) shows an active earthwork/construction site (graded building pads, haul roads, retention/detention ponds) with no completed building. Under-construction / not-yet-built hyperscale, so no building to place a point on; coords left null.

ColoCrossing SJ1 at 55 S Market Street is an in-building colocation inside a downtown San Jose office building, not a standalone data-center box. Census geocoder exact-matched the address to the specific building. Satellite is dense downtown grid with no distinct DC roof signature (expected for in-building colo). Point placed on the geocoded building footprint.

Flexential Tampa/West (TPA01/02), 31,600 sqft colo, address 9417 Corporate Lake Dr confirmed via operator page and datacenterHawk/Inflect. Census geocoded the address exactly. Satellite shows two near-identical low-rise flat-roofed flex/office buildings with parking and small rooftop HVAC units in a corporate office park. This is an in-building colo occupying part of one building; identity certain but minor building-level ambiguity between the two matching buildings. Point placed on the western building rooftop centroid at the registered address.

Evocative Emeryville EM1/OAK1, 1400 65th St Suite 150, ~22,500 sqft in-building colo. Address confirmed via datacenters.com and Inflect; census geocoded exactly. Satellite shows a large flat-roofed industrial building (with rooftop solar array) in dense Emeryville industrial district at the geocoded point. In-building colo within a multi-tenant industrial building; point placed on that building's rooftop centroid.

Netrality Indy Telcom Center 733 W Henry St, ~30,000 sqft (former Lifeline). Confirmed via Netrality operator page and PeeringDB fac/186. Census geocoded 733 W Henry exactly. Satellite clearly shows the large multi-building Indy Telcom industrial/telecom campus (former Western Electric complex) with big dark-roofed buildings. Multi-building campus (Netrality also runs 701 W Henry here) so exact building among several is not fully distinguishable from imagery; point placed on the central campus building at the geocoded address.

Level(3)/Lumen New York Gateway 2 at 85 Tenth Ave (former Nabisco factory carrier hotel, 88,354 sqft). Address confirmed via Baxtel and DCD. Census geocoded 85 10th Ave exactly. Satellite shows a large full-block early-1900s building on the east side of 10th Ave in Chelsea matching the known carrier-hotel footprint. Dense Manhattan block; multi-tenant carrier hotel so exact rooftop centroid within the block carries minor uncertainty. Point placed on the identified full-block building.

T5@Atlanta hyperscale campus, 1456 Trae Lane, Lithia Springs. Confirmed via datacentermap and Inflect. Census geocoded address. Satellite unambiguously shows a data center: large light-colored windowless box with dense rows of rooftop cooling units, an adjacent generator/container yard (rows of units, several red), and a circular retention pond. Geocode landed on woods/road; point MOVED onto the DC building rooftop centroid. Multi-building T5 campus but this building unambiguously belongs to the 1456 Trae Lane facility.

Direct LTx (Directlink Technologies) Reading, 2561 Bernville Rd, claimed 300,000 sqft / 110,000 raised floor. Address confirmed via datacenters.com and datacenterHawk; census geocoded exactly. Satellite shows a large dark-roofed multi-wing building with extensive surrounding parking at the exact address, but no strong external DC signals (no visible large cooling array, substation, or generator yard) - consistent with a converted office/corporate building housing colo. No competing candidate seen, so point placed on that building's centroid, but visual DC confirmation is weak.

Centersquare/Csquare Boston BOS4-A at 486 Arsenal Way, Watertown (368,000 sqft, 31 MW, ex-Cyxtera/Evoque). Identity confirmed via datacenters.com and PeeringDB fac/6678. However census matched to '486 Arsenal St' (a different street than Arsenal Way), landing at a road intersection. The area is the large multi-building 'Arsenal on the Charles'/Arsenal Yards campus; the most prominent nearby building has full rooftop solar (atypical for a data center that needs roof cooling space), and no building shows clear DC signals. Cannot confidently pin the specific 368k sqft DC building; coords left null.

H5 Data Centers Quincy WA, 240,000 sqft purpose-built facility acquired from Intuit in 2018 (Intuit remains a tenant) - matches the record name 'H5 Data Center', operator Intuit, WA. Confirmed via H5 operator page and DCD. No street address in dataset but provided coords sit in Quincy's data-center cluster. Satellite at the coords shows a classic large windowless data-center box with a fenced yard and open land, plus another DC visible on the right edge. Point placed on the coord-centered DC building; medium because Quincy has several look-alike hyperscale DCs nearby.

Crown Castle Pittsburgh (Allegheny Center Mall), 100 South Commons Suite 142, small interconnection colo (17,287 sqft footprint, 100 kW). Confirmed via DataCenterJournal, PeeringDB fac/1423, DataBank. Census geocoded 100 S Commons. Satellite shows the distinctive large Allegheny Center superblock complex on Pittsburgh's North Side at the geocode. In-building/in-mall colo; point placed on that complex's building centroid. Small suite within a large mixed-use complex, so building identified but suite-level precision is inherent to such colos.

CyrusOne Hamilton (CIN4), 103 Knightsbridge Dr, 83,000 sqft / 9 MW. Identity/address confirmed via datacenters.com, Inflect, PeeringDB fac/1471; census geocoded 103 Knightsbridge exactly. Satellite shows a business-park setting near a river with a large white-roofed building (semicircular drive) near the geocoded point plus other buildings, but no unambiguous DC signals (no clear rooftop chiller array or generator yard). Point placed on the most prominent building nearest the address; low confidence due to weak visual confirmation and some building ambiguity.

Level(3)/Lumen Boise at 435 W McGregor Dr (dataset status was already ambiguous_candidate). Identity/address confirmed via DataCenterJournal and Baxtel (Lumen Boise 1). Census geocoded to a point at a canal/road. Satellite shows a cluster of small light-industrial buildings south of the canal, one with possible rooftop telecom equipment, but none is clearly identifiable as the specific Level3 network node versus neighbors. Cannot confidently pick the building; coords left null.

Aunalytics South Bend (AUSB, formerly Data Realty), 1440 Ignition Dr S, 48,000 sqft / 2.4 MW, located in Ignition Park (former Studebaker site). Confirmed via datacentermap and PeeringDB fac/9309. Census could not geocode the newer street; used dataset coords 41.661423,-86.257342. Satellite at those coords shows Ignition Park with a distinctive modern building cluster (angular canopy/solar feature, rooftop mechanical) matching Data Realty's known architecture. Point placed on that building's rooftop centroid; medium because exact cooling signature is not fully resolvable at this zoom.

Digital Realty IAD39, 44274 Round Table Plaza Bldg L, Ashburn VA 20147 (~1M sqft campus). IMPORTANT DATA ERROR: the dataset/PeeringDB coordinates (32.9587,-96.8221) point to Dallas TX, not Ashburn — PeeringDB itself carries the bad geocode. Independently re-geocoded via OSM Nominatim to an entry explicitly labeled 'Digital Realty Northern Virginia IAD39' at 39.0076,-77.4675. Esri satellite at that point shows a massive white-roofed data-center campus: extensive rooftop cooling arrays, security fencing/perimeter road, adjacent electrical substation at the south edge, retention pond. Point placed on the main IAD39 building. Multi-building campus so exact 'Bldg L' among 2 large buildings is a minor ambiguity, hence CANDIDATE not VERIFIED.

Syringa Networks HQ/colo, 3795 S Development Ave, Boise ID 83705. Identity confirmed (operator HQ at this address). Census geocoded cleanly to 43.5677,-116.2075 but the point lands on the Development Ave roadway. Satellite shows a small industrial/office business park with several visually similar tan/white flat-roofed buildings and no distinctive data-center signal (no rooftop cooling array, substation, or security fencing distinguishable). Cannot isolate the specific Syringa building among multiple look-alike candidates -> AMBIGUOUS.

San Diego Supercomputer Center, UC San Diego (9836 Hopkins Dr, La Jolla). Census could not geocode the campus address; used the Wikidata-derived suggested coords 32.8840,-117.2393. Satellite confirms the point is on the eastern edge of the UCSD campus adjacent to open canyon/eucalyptus land, which matches SDSC's known location. It is a supercomputer housed inside a normal research building (not a windowless DC box), so the exact building is not distinctive from imagery -> CANDIDATE.

Digital Realty CLT (former Telx), 125 N Myers St, Charlotte NC 28202 — an uptown carrier hotel. Census geocoded to 35.2215,-80.8358 on a building in the dense downtown grid; satellite shows a mid-rise urban building with rooftop mechanical equipment at the point. Urban in-building colo with no distinctive satellite signal, but the address geocodes onto the correct building -> CANDIDATE.

Aureon West Des Moines, 1661 Westown Pkwy, listed as the 'Westown Communications Center' (1.5 MW). Zoomed Esri satellite at the geocoded point shows a windowless flat-roofed building with three distinct rooftop HVAC/cooling units and a fenced gravel equipment yard — consistent with a telco/data-center building — sitting alone at the address. Identity + building clearly seen + point on it + no competing look-alike -> VERIFIED.

Midco Grand Forks dual-purpose headend/data center (~6,500 sqft, opened 2018). Source address conflict: PeeringDB/ocolo list 4301 18th Ave S while datacentermap lists 3251 32nd Ave S. Census geocode of 4301 18th Ave S (47.9026,-97.0883) lands on open farmland at the developing SW edge of town; the suggested coords to the west show a rural business park with several small buildings (one bright white newish building is a possible candidate) but no clearly distinctive data-center/headend signal to confirm. Address conflict + multiple indistinguishable small buildings -> AMBIGUOUS.

Stream Data Centers MN I, 1708 W Creek Ln, Chaska MN. Census geocoded to 44.8061,-93.6348 which landed on a road; the dataset's suggested coords pointed north but overshot into a field. Re-fetching centered between the two revealed a large white windowless data-center building at ~44.8084,-93.6341 with a full security perimeter road, rooftop cooling units, loading docks, and a satellite dish on the west side. Point moved onto the building's rooftop centroid. Clear DC identity and building -> VERIFIED.

Digital Realty SFO '365 Main', 365 Main Street, San Francisco — a well-known carrier hotel. Census geocoded to 37.7886,-122.3908, landing on the correct downtown SoMa building one block from the Bay Bridge (bridge/water visible in imagery). Dense urban colo with no distinctive rooftop signal from above, but the named building is unambiguous and the geocode is on it -> CANDIDATE.

The Garland Center / West 7 Center, 1200 W 7th St, downtown Los Angeles — a large multi-story carrier hotel. Census geocoded to 34.0514,-118.2658 on a large light-roofed building with rooftop mechanical equipment in the downtown core. Known named building at the confirmed address; urban colo not distinctive from above -> CANDIDATE.

Burbank Water and Power (municipal utility carrier hotel/EGIX), 164 W Magnolia Blvd, Burbank CA. Census geocoded to 34.1786,-118.3164. Satellite confirms the BWP campus identity — utility infrastructure to the SE including large round tanks and an electrical switchyard/substation — but the geocoded address point sits on a campus/commercial building on Magnolia and the specific building housing the data center within the multi-building utility campus is not distinctly identifiable. Correct operator/campus, uncertain exact building -> CANDIDATE (low).

75 Broad Street, 19th Floor, New York NY — an in-building colocation within the historic 75 Broad St office tower (former Western Union/ITT building) in the Financial District. Census geocoded to 40.7044,-74.0115 onto the correct tower in the dense FiDi grid. In-building colo (specific floor) so the correct tower is identified but the DC is one tenant inside a multi-tenant building -> CANDIDATE.

EdgeConneX Richmond EDCRIC01, 1450 E Parham Road, Suite 1450, Richmond VA — a small edge facility (16,279 sqft total, 0.5 MW, built 2015). Census geocoded 1450 E Parham to 37.6386,-77.4682 near the road; the large multi-tenant building housing Suite 1450 sits just NE, confirmed by re-fetch at the suggested coords (building appears at frame edge in both fetches, centroid ~37.6391,-77.4677). It is a colo suite inside a larger commercial building with heavy surface parking (not a standalone windowless DC), so building identified but occupancy is a suite -> CANDIDATE.

Evocative DAL6 (formerly Internap/INAP Dallas), 111,000 sqft colo at 1221 Coit Rd, Plano TX, confirmed via datacentermap/datacenters listings. Census geocode landed on Coit Rd. Esri satellite shows a large windowless flat-roofed box on the west side of Coit Rd (lower-left of frame) consistent with the ~111k sqft data center footprint; moved point onto that building's rooftop centroid, which matches the pipeline's suggested coordinate (33.0152,-96.7687). No definitive rooftop cooling visible at this resolution and it sits in an office park, hence CANDIDATE.

Evocative JFK2 (38,000 sqft) at 480 N Bedford Rd, Chappaqua NY. Census geocode for the street number fell ~0.6mi east into woods (interpolation error). The real 480 (N) Bedford Road is the landmark Chappaqua Crossing campus (former Reader's Digest HQ); satellite at the pipeline's suggested coords clearly shows the large Georgian office complex with adjacent athletic field. Placed point on the main building. Specific wing housing the colo is not distinguishable, so CANDIDATE not VERIFIED.

Hurricane Electric San Jose 1 is an in-building colocation (Suite 1400) in the historic MAE West building at 55 S Market St, downtown San Jose, per PeeringDB fac/16184 and inflect listing. Census geocode resolves to the 55 S Market address in the dense downtown core; placed point on the office tower at that corner. In-building colo in a specific tower = CANDIDATE per criteria.

H5 Data Centers Quincy II/III, 452,300 sqft / 63-acre campus at 1500 M St NE, Quincy WA. Quincy hosts many operators (Microsoft, Sabey, Vantage, H5) with adjacent look-alike buildings; I could not reliably distinguish H5's specific II/III buildings from neighbors, so left coordinates unset.

TierPoint Sioux Falls West, 25,000 sqft at 5300 N La Mesa Dr, described as 'tucked into the landscape' with on-site generators. Census geocode fell on the adjacent highway. Unmistakable single facility with no look-alike; moved point onto the building.

ScaleMatrix Dallas US-South 01 (16,000 sqft) at 820 Allen Commerce Pkwy, Allen TX. Research (datacenterHawk) shows 820 Allen Commerce Pkwy is a two-building purpose-built asset jointly occupied by TierPoint DA2 and ScaleMatrix under the same street address. Satellite shows two adjacent data-center buildings ~150m apart: a large box with an extensive rooftop cooling farm (E) and a solar-roofed building with a ground mechanical yard (W). Both are data centers; could not confirm which one is ScaleMatrix vs TierPoint, so left coordinates unset.

Centersquare DFW3 / Evoque DA2 (140,453 sqft, Suite 200) at 11800 (also listed 11830) Webb Chapel Rd, Dallas TX. Census geocode landed on Webb Chapel Rd in a retail corridor. Nearest large non-retail structure on the west side is a big building with extensive rooftop solar and two rounded structures; consistent with a multi-tenant building housing a colo 'Suite 200'. Office-like appearance and the 11800/11830 address discrepancy keep this at CANDIDATE/low.

BOTHWAXB Bothell Central Office (Lumen/CenturyLink telecom CO, CLLI BOTHWAXB) at 2326 228th St SE, Bothell WA, per PeeringDB fac/14272. Census geocode resolves to 228th St SE; Esri satellite shows a single small windowless building isolated among trees just south of the road with a small equipment/parking yard, exactly at the address and with no competing commercial structure in the residential surroundings. Placed point on the building.

Midco Sioux Falls, 11,000 sqft (9,000 raised floor) at 5401 S Solberg Ave, per datacentermap/solberg listing. Midco is a cable/broadband operator; satellite shows a technical building with an adjacent white satellite radome (headend signature) on the NW part of the campus, matching the pipeline's independent suggested coordinate, plus a larger office building with heavy parking to the south. Centersquare/Cyxtera Tampa TPA1, 96,000 sqft single-story TPO-membrane-roof DC at 9310 Florida Palm Dr, Tampa FL. Census geocode fell in scrub just south. GRHMORXB Gresham Central Office, 150 NW 20th St, telco central office / colo (PeeringDB fac/14267, now WholeSail/Ziply, formerly Zayo). Ark Data Centers (formerly Involta) Duluth 2, 421 N 6th Ave E, ~12,000 sqft. Census geocoded to a specific downtown Duluth building; converted building is not visually distinguishable from neighbors in aerial. Point on geocoded address.

Network 222 (Fiore Companies), 222 W Washington Ave, 3MW colo inside a ten-story, ~190,000 sqft downtown Madison office building (near the WI State Capitol, visible in aerial). In-building colo; correct office tower identified via geocoded address. Point on geocoded address.

NOCIX Clay, 201 E 16th Ave, North Kansas City, ~24,000 sqft modified building at 16th & Clay, 1.5MW. Census geocoded the address into a dense industrial district of many look-alike flat-roof warehouses; a large flat-roof building sits at the address but cannot be uniquely confirmed as the DC from aerial. Point on geocoded address.

iConnect Billings Fiber Hotel resolves to Granite Tower, 222 N 32nd St Suite 301, Billings (PeeringDB fac/380). This is an in-building colo/carrier hotel inside a downtown office tower. Original record only had 'Granite Tower' as address; I geocoded the resolved street address (222 N 32nd St). Aerial shows downtown Billings office blocks; correct tower is the address building. Point on geocoded address.

CENTRA PWM1, 340 Cumberland Ave, Portland ME (PeeringDB fac/11838) - the primary carrier-neutral telecom hotel in Maine, home to NNENIX exchange, ~3MW. In-building colo inside a downtown Portland building. Census geocoded the address; a large flat-roofed building sits at the address. Point on geocoded address.

DataBank DFW3, 8375 Dominion Pkwy, Plano, ~144,000 sqft purpose-built (2018), 13.5MW, with attached DFW8 expansion, on a 16-acre campus. Building not uniquely confirmable from aerial. Point on geocoded street address.

Binary.net 'The Vault', 134 S 13th St, downtown Lincoln - colo built inside a former Federal Reserve Bank vault (30-inch concrete walls, 30-ton vault door) within a larger downtown building, ~10,000 sqft raised floor, 1MW. In-building colo; correct downtown building identified via geocoded address. Point on geocoded address.

KDWEB San Jose is an in-building colocation/interconnection suite on the 18th floor of the office tower at 50 W San Fernando St, downtown San Jose (confirmed by DataCenterMap, Inflect, Newby Ventures listings). Census geocode landed on the correct downtown office block; satellite shows the multi-story office building at the address. Being an in-building colo in an identified multi-tenant tower, correct building identified but no standalone DC signals to fully verify.

Cogent Cleveland (CLE04) is an in-building colo inside the historic 21-story Keith Building at 1621 Euclid Ave in Playhouse Square (confirmed by DataCenterMap, Inflect, Cogent's own page). Geocode landed exactly on the Keith Building block downtown; satellite confirms a large downtown office tower at the address. In-building colo in an identified tower.

Prov.net/Alpha3 Cloud Providence 1 is a small 4,000 sqft in-building colo at 935 Westminster St, Providence (confirmed by DataCenterMap, datacenters.com). Geocode matched the exact address; satellite shows a small commercial building block in the Westminster St corridor. Identity and address confirmed; small in-building colo so no standalone DC signals visible.

Iron Mountain NJE-1, a purpose-built 830,000 sqft / 30MW data center on a 43-acre campus at 3003 Woodbridge Ave, Edison NJ. Satellite unmistakably shows a massive windowless data-center building whose roof is almost entirely covered by a dense grid of solar panels, matching NJE-1's documented 18,000-module / 7.2MW rooftop solar array (largest of any NA data center). Building adjacent to highway, no competing look-alike. Point placed on the solar-covered rooftop centroid.

Encore Technologies MSB1 at 9333 N Springboro Pike, Miamisburg OH is a 73,632 sqft building (built 1980) operated as LexisNexis' dedicated data center (confirmed by DataCenterMap, LoopNet, DCD reporting on the Encore/4620 Wesley LLC purchase). Geocode landed on Springboro Pike; the nearest matching building is a campus structure on the west side of the road. Address and identity confirmed but the building reads as an office-style campus building with no distinctive windowless-box/rooftop-cooling DC signature at this resolution, so not fully verified.

Ethoplex 'Edge DC - Fond du Lac' record is internally inconsistent and cannot be pinned to a building. The dataset street address (W6720 Subway Rd, Friendship WI 54937) is in Adams County, ~50 miles NW of Fond du Lac; the facility name says Fond du Lac; and Ethoplex's only PeeringDB-listed data center is at N115W19150 Edison Dr, Germantown WI 53022. No public source gives a street address or identifiable building for a Fond du Lac edge node. Conflicting sources, no verifiable building -> unresolvable.

DQE Communications Allegheny Center Mall is a 17,287 sqft in-building colo at 100 South Commons, Pittsburgh (confirmed by Inflect, DataCenterJournal, Baxtel). Correct complex identified; specific colo building within the large multi-building complex not individually distinguishable, so in-building CANDIDATE.

DataBank Orangeburg is a 34-acre campus at 2000 Corporate Drive that hosts BOTH LGA3 (110,000 sqft, opened May 2025) and LGA4 under the same address (confirmed by DataBank, DCD, datacenterHawk). With two named facilities sharing one address and no public floor plan to distinguish LGA3 from LGA4/construction, the specific LGA3 building cannot be reliably isolated.

DataBank DEN1 (formerly Latisys 'Data 393', later Zayo zColo) is a 53,200 sqft data center at 393 Inverness Parkway, Englewood CO (confirmed by DataBank datasheet at that address, Baxtel, datacenterHawk). Point placed on the building adjacent to the geocoded address.

Digital Realty 200 Paul Avenue (SFO10/SFR1) is the well-known San Francisco carrier hotel, a 482,000 sqft 5-story data center on a 7.35-acre multi-building site ~4 miles south of downtown (confirmed by Digital Realty, DataCenterMap, Baxtel). Census point-geocode landed just south of the complex; imagery to the north shows the large data-center buildings with rows of dedicated rooftop cooling/HVAC equipment arrays characteristic of a major data hall, no competing look-alike. Point moved onto the main data-center building's rooftop centroid.

Atomic Data 'Minneapolis Central' (MSP250) is an in-building colo at 250 S Marquette Ave in the downtown Minneapolis core (confirmed by PeeringDB, Inflect, datacenters.com). In-building colo in an identified tower, no standalone DC signals to fully verify.

EdgeConneX San Diego SDG01 is a small edge colo (retrofit, ~0.5MW) occupying Suite 150B at 5761 Copley Drive, Kearny Mesa, San Diego (confirmed by datacenterHawk, EdgeConneX San Diego datasheet, DataCenterMap). Geocode landed at 5761 Copley Dr among flex/industrial buildings; the suite is inside a commercial building rather than a standalone DC, so the specific building at the address is identified but no distinctive DC signature is visible. Point placed on the building nearest the geocoded suite address.

Identity: Quest Technology Management 'High Availability Business Center' (HABC), operator HQ at 9000 Foothills Blvd, Roseville CA (questsys.com; datacenters.com listing). Esri satellite shows a large corporate campus: an office building with wings plus a large central rectangular building. Data center is an in-building colo within this HABC building, not a standalone windowless DC, so CANDIDATE. Point moved off the road onto the main central building.

Identity: DC BLOX HSV1, 13,000 SF Tier-3 data center at 333 Diamond Drive NW, Huntsville AL (dcblox.com; datacentermap). Census geocode matched exact address. Identity: UPX Miami is listed at 16901 Collins Avenue, Unit 3601, Sunny Isles Beach FL (PeeringDB fac/6529; UPX US LLC). No verifiable DC building -> coords left null.

Identity: Ark Data Centers (formerly Involta) Marion/Cedar Rapids, 19,000 SF, 5055 Rec Dr, Marion IA (arkdna.com; datacentermap). Census geocode matched the address and landed at a rural crossroads. Cannot confidently isolate which structure is the 19,000 SF data center -> AMBIGUOUS. Best-guess coords placed on the nearest flat-roofed commercial building to the addressed point.

Identity: iM Data Centers Miami (IM Interconnect) occupies the 7th floor of the Digital Realty/DataBank carrier hotel at 36 NE 2nd Street, downtown Miami FL (Digital Realty MIA10; inflect.com). Point placed on the 36 NE 2nd St building at the geocoded address.

Identity: BLDR-1919 corresponds to 1919 14th St, Boulder CO 80302 - 'The Colorado Building', the tallest office building in downtown Boulder (now Industrious coworking; wwreynolds.com). Identity: Expedient Pittsburgh - Nova Place, a ~26,000 SF colo/cloud data center at 1 Allegheny Center, Pittsburgh PA (North Side), inside Nova Place - the former Allegheny Center Mall, now a large mixed-use complex (expedient.com; Post-Gazette). Identity: Stream Data Centers DFW VII, a greenfield hyperscale campus (275,000 SF, 43 MW, ~23 acres) at 1813 Lookout Dr, Garland TX, with a documented 95 MW on-site Oncor substation and fully leased to a major cloud customer in 2023 (streamdatacenters.com; datacenterhawk). Identity: XO/Verizon '600 West 7th' data center occupies the 3rd floor of the 7-story West 7th Center (W7C) carrier hotel at 600 West 7th St, downtown Los Angeles CA - one of the best-connected buildings in LA after One Wilshire (west7center.com; datacentermap; PeeringDB fac/1004). Identity: INOC ALB1, a small ~4,000 SF colo/meet-me facility at 80 State Street, downtown Albany NY (inoc.net; datacentermap; PeeringDB fac/1291). Census geocode matched the exact address. Identity: Rochelle Technology Center at 910 Technology Parkway, Rochelle IL 61068, in the Rochelle Business & Technology Park; address is Follett Corporation, which operated a data center there (rmu.net; cityofrochelle.net). QTS JCY1 is an in-building colo occupying floors 16-18 of a 19-story building at 95 Christopher Columbus Drive, Jersey City (120,000 sqft, 3MW). CyrusOne CIN5 (Lebanon OH). Clackamas Education Service District operates a small ~3,000 sqft data center / colocation within its facility (the 'Sunnybrook' site) at 13455 SE 97th Ave, Clackamas OR. CyrusOne NYM7 at 155 Myers Corners Rd, Wappingers Falls NY (100,000 sqft, 37,000 sqft raised floor, up to 10MW). SDN Communications data center at Mark Shlanta Technology Park, 5300 N La Mesa Dr, NW of Sioux Falls (purpose-built 2012, 12-inch concrete walls, colocation/DR). DataBank MSP3 at 8111 Oxbow Creek Dr N, Brooklyn Park MN (opened 2021, 82,231 sqft, 9MW, 14-acre campus with room to expand). Point on the building rooftop centroid.

Evocative LAX14 (formerly Internap LAX014) at 3690 Redondo Beach Ave, Redondo Beach CA (one-story structural steel/concrete DC built 2012, 104,000 sqft, 7MW). Point on the rooftop centroid of the cooling-unit building.

ColoCenter.com Manchester (now FirstLight Fiber) is an in-building colo on the 2nd floor at 77 Sundial Avenue, Manchester NH (14,400 sqft, 4MW). It is a suite inside that building, not a standalone DC. Point placed on the large building; specific floor/suite not distinguishable.

Cologix DSM1 is a carrier-hotel colo located inside the Financial Center building at 666 Walnut Street, downtown Des Moines (4,000 sqft meet-me-room, DesMoinesIX). Quad State Internet PAH1 is a small ISP colocation / light-colo at 1212 Helen St, Paducah KY (carrier-neutral, hosts a Paducah IX and a Hurricane Electric PoP). FirstLight 'Regen Hut' (PeeringDB fac/3860) is a long-haul fiber regeneration/interconnection hut in the Portland ME metro, not a conventional data-center building. DataBank IND1 is a 54,000 sqft downtown carrier hotel at 731 W Henry St in the telecom-dense Henry Street area (databank.com confirms address). DartPoints BTR1 (7127 Florida Blvd) is inside the Bon Carre Technology Park, a former shopping-mall complex (86,400 sqft, confirmed by datacenterHawk/DCD). EdgeConneX Portland EDCPOR01 is 'Building C, Suite C-300' at 6327 NE/NW Evergreen Parkway, Hillsboro, part of a multi-building corporate park also hosting EDCPOR02 (datacenterHawk). CENTRA CLT01 (formerly Deep Edge Realty) is an interconnection/colocation facility inside the building at 701 E Trade St, Charlotte NC, confirmed by PeeringDB (fac/7878), datacentermap and inflect listings. In-building colo in an office tower, so no rooftop-DC signals; identity firm from address + named landmark. Point on the geocoded building.

Fiber Alley operates edge data centers at 8915/8917 Complex Dr in the Kearny Mesa business park, San Diego. Census geocode matched 8915 Complex Dr and lands among a row of nearly identical flat-roofed light-industrial/commercial buildings. No distinctive DC signal (cooling array/substation) visible; the specific edge-DC suite cannot be uniquely picked out from look-alike neighbors. Point placed on the flat-roofed building fronting Complex Dr nearest the geocode.

'Hub Express System' (HEX) is not a data center building: it is TOWARDEX/TWDX Infrastructure's open-access underground fiber conduit and vault system (a 'Joint Use Communications Trench') running ~1 km beneath the streets of Somerville's Inner Belt, with rectangular concrete vaults and 24-30x 4in conduit banks. The listing address '0 Inner Belt Rd' is a placeholder for linear underground infrastructure. There is no discrete building to place a point on -> UNRESOLVABLE.

Flexential SLC05 (former ViaWest) is at 6340 S 3000 E Ste 510, Cottonwood Heights. Census geocode landed at the 3000 E / ~6200 S road intersection. Surrounding imagery is a mix of a large retail/mall structure (NW), garden-style apartment complexes (E, with pools), and small commercial buildings (NE, SW); none shows an unambiguous data-center signature and the exact building cannot be confidently distinguished among the several candidates. Leaving coords null.

DartPoints Spartanburg SPA1 is described as a purpose-built stand-alone facility (~21,400 sqft) at 5700 N Blackstock Rd. Census geocode matched the address; satellite (zoomed) shows a single white flat-roofed windowless building with small rooftop mechanical units, its own perimeter parking, and a large ground-mounted solar array immediately adjacent to the SE - a strong DC signature with no competing look-alike. Point placed on the building rooftop centroid.

Digital Realty LAX12 is a 2-story, ~132,000 sqft dedicated data center at 2260 E El Segundo Blvd, El Segundo. Census geocode matched the address (SE of the El Segundo Blvd intersection). Zoomed satellite shows a large gray windowless building with a visible row of rooftop cooling units directly at that corner - matching a dedicated DC. Original 'no_building_found_manual' status upgraded; point moved off the road onto the building rooftop.

LCOG Park Place is a carrier-neutral interconnection facility (carrier hotel) operated by the Lane Council of Governments in the basement and 2nd floor of the Park Place building at 859 Willamette St, downtown Eugene (LCOG's own offices; ~20-23 cabinets). Census geocode matched the address on the correct downtown block. It is explicitly an in-building interconnection room, not a standalone server hall, so no external DC signals; identity firm from address + operator page. Point on the geocoded building.

Fireline Broadband runs a small (~1 MW, 100-ton) NOC/data center at 9911 Norwalk Blvd, Santa Fe Springs. Census geocode matched the address at a Norwalk Blvd intersection surrounded by near-identical commercial/industrial buildings on all corners. No distinctive DC signature; the exact single-tenant building could not be visually confirmed. Point placed on the commercial building at the address frontage; low confidence.

200 Quannapowitt Pkwy, Wakefield is a ~218,000 sqft former Digital Realty data center (now AXCELX colo, 24 MW). Census geocode matched 200 Quannapowitt and lands on a dark-roofed office-style building with multiple white rooftop HVAC units by Lake Quannapowitt. Building is DC-consistent but presents as an office building and sits beside a near-identical look-alike (100 Quannapowitt / former Comverse complex), so the exact building is not unambiguous. Point on the geocoded building.

Alpheus Austin (now LOGIX Fiber Networks) is a small in-building colo (~12,000 gross / 3,000 usable sqft, Suite 150, 2.4 MW) at 1905 E 6th St, East Austin. Point on the geocoded building.

Telehouse New York Teleport is at 7 Teleport Drive within The Teleport office park, Staten Island. Census geocode matched 7 Teleport Dr; the point sat in open ground, so it was moved WSW onto the actual building. Zoomed satellite shows a large windowless flat-roofed building in an isolated, fully-fenced secure compound with rows of rooftop mechanical units and a dedicated parking lot set apart in woodland - an unmistakable data-center signature with no competing structure. Point on the building rooftop centroid.

Level(3)/Lumen Atlanta Peachtree is a carrier-neutral in-building colocation on the 3rd/4th floors of the office tower at 180 Peachtree St NW, downtown Atlanta (datacentermap, inflect).
