Not a standalone DC so no rooftop cooling signature to confirm; point placed on the geocoded building.

Ethoplex Edge DC Green Bay at claimed 4151 Heritage Rd, De Pere WI could not be confirmed. Ethoplex's documented facilities are in Germantown/SE Wisconsin, not De Pere (ethoplex.com, peeringdb). No identifiable DC building at either location; small edge micro-DC with no public building info. Coords left null.

QTS Atlanta-Suwanee (SUW1) at 300 Satellite Blvd is a 370,000 sqft tilt-up concrete data center sited in a wooded valley (datacenters.com, datacentermap). Census geocode landed on the entrance road/intersection; point moved onto the building's rooftop centroid.

DQE Communications Downtown Pittsburgh is a small (~3,000 sqft, 1.2 MW) in-building colocation at 322 4th Ave, Pittsburgh (datacentermap, peeringdb fac/8125). Colo@34 (Performive/CloudFirst) is an in-building colocation at 34 Peachtree St, downtown Atlanta, a bandwidth-rich carrier location near the downtown Telx POP (datacenterjournal, datacentermap). H5 Data Centers Tampa (TA1) is a 27,300 sqft in-building colo occupying Suite 1000 of the Franklin Exchange building, a distinctive downtown Tampa high-rise (once the city's tallest), at 655 N Franklin St (h5datacenters.com, datacenterhawk). CENTRA CMH01 (formerly Deep Edge) is a 2 MW in-building interconnection colo on the B1 level of the Borden Building, a 438-ft skyscraper at 180 E Broad St, Columbus OH (baxtel, datacentermap, peeringdb fac/12210). Equinix LA4 (paired with LA3) is a single-story tilt-up concrete 177,469 sqft data center at 445 N Douglas St, El Segundo (equinix.com, datacentermap, datacenterhawk). Equinix PH1 is a carrier-neutral in-building colo (Suite 990, ~26,780 sqft) inside the 11-story commercial building at 401 N Broad Street, downtown Philadelphia. NP Rochester is a small PeeringDB-listed interconnection/colo facility at 220 S Broadway, downtown Rochester MN. MDC1 (Colocation America, also DataBank/Digital Realty MIA10) is a ~12,000 sqft in-building colo at 36 NE 2nd St, Suite 400, downtown Miami, near the NAP of the Americas. Metanet Chelsea is a ~60,000 sqft in-building colo at 85 Tenth Avenue, Manhattan (Chelsea, one block from the Hudson, visible in image). 85 Tenth Ave is a large full-block telecom/office building. IC2NET is a ~2.5 MW in-building colo at 14351 Myford Rd, Tustin, in a large business/industrial park. Data Holdings Milwaukee, 3135 W Highland Blvd, is a purpose-built two-story hardened Tier III+ data center (46,000 sqft, 8 MW). Venyu BTR1 (now DartPoints), 7127 Florida Blvd, is a 34,000 sqft DC floor within the ~47,000 sqft Bon Carre Business Center (a large former-mall complex) in Baton Rouge. Fusion Connect Cleveland (fka Fidelity Voice and Data), 1621 Euclid Ave, is an in-building colo inside the historic 21-story Keith Building at Playhouse Square, downtown Cleveland (within an H5 data-center building). Cloudpath CT19, 5 Landmark Square, Stamford CT, is a 450 kW in-building colo within the Landmark Square office complex (Cloudpath's own contact is 6 Landmark Square, next door). DNSnetworks IDC4 is an in-building colocation/interconnection facility at 36 NE 2nd St, a ~160,000 sqft downtown Miami carrier hotel (confirmed via PeeringDB fac/9707 and datacentermap). MDC Nogales (NOG1) is a small carrier-neutral colo (0.2-1MW) at 81 N Sonoita Ave, confirmed via MDC Data Centers site and datacentermap. Census geocoded the address cleanly to 31.335472,-110.943007. XMission Salt Lake City is a carrier-neutral in-building colo (hosts the SLIX exchange) at 67 E 400 S, confirmed via PeeringDB fac/1335 and inflect. Atlantech Online is an 8,000 sqft Tier-2 in-building colo occupying space in a multi-tenant building at 1010 Wayne Ave, downtown Silver Spring, operating there since 1998 (confirmed via datacentermap and atlantech.net). AUBix Auburn is a purpose-built 40,000 sqft standalone data center on a 6-acre site at 1571 W Samford Ave (confirmed via datacentermap, aubix.net, AL Governor grand-opening release; now leased by Core Scientific). H5 Data Centers Buffalo II is a 409,200 sqft Tier III data center on 43 acres in Lockport NY, the former Yahoo! Lockport facility (confirmed via datacentermap and H5 site). Foremost Corpus Christi is a carrier interconnection point (PeeringDB fac/12069, CLLI CRCHTX93W48) located in Suite 822 of the downtown office tower at 606 N Carancahua St. Census geocoded the address to 27.795751,-97.397393 on the correct downtown block. Meta Los Lunas campus at 4250 Messenger Loop NW (Meta's own site + DCD). Cortex is an in-facility supercluster inside/adjacent to the factory rather than a standalone DC building, so the exact hall is not independently visually confirmable.

AWS Hermiston OR (record: 976 East Penney Avenue). Esri imagery shows an unmistakable data center: a large windowless building block with dense rooftop cooling-unit arrays and, immediately adjacent (lower-left), a dedicated fenced electrical substation with multiple transformers. Point sits on the DC building. The dedicated substation plus rooftop cooling confirm DC use and there is one clear main building block at this point.

QTS Atlanta-Suwanee GA (listed address 300 Satellite Blvd). Conflicting sources: the record coordinate and the census geocode of 300 Satellite Blvd differ by ~800m. Imagery at the record coord shows the point in a wooded gap between two large flat-roofed buildings; imagery at the geocoded address shows an intersection with scattered office/commercial buildings. No single unambiguous DC building (windowless box + cooling/substation) is identifiable at either location, so the exact building cannot be bound.

CenturyLink/Lumen CA2 at 3015 Winona Avenue, Burbank CA. Census geocode of that address (34.199321, -118.343534) is ~60m from the record coordinate, confirming identity. Coords moved to the geocoded building. This is an in-building telecom colocation facility in a dense urban Burbank industrial block; the specific building is known from the published address but is not visually distinguishable from surrounding buildings in satellite imagery.

LightEdge Austin II at 7000B Burleson Road, Austin TX. Census geocoder returned an exact rooftop-level match for 7000B Burleson Rd (30.19944, -97.70710). Imagery shows a commercial/industrial business park with large flat-roofed buildings. This is an in-building colocation suite ('B' unit) within a multi-tenant park, so the correct building is known from the published address but the specific colo space is not visually confirmable.

Second AWS Boardman OR record on the same campus, ~150m north of dc_fa46f751ca71 with the identical shared address 'Lewis and Clark Drive'. Imagery shows the same hyperscale DC campus (windowless boxes, rooftop cooling arrays, green retention pond). Point sits on a DC building, but the shared single address across multiple identical buildings (and a second record for the same campus) makes the specific building unbindable.

NTT Ashburn VA7, 44371 Gigabit Plaza. NTT states VA7 becomes available September 2026 (i.e. still under construction). Esri imagery confirms this: the parcel at the coordinate is a bare graded dirt lot in the center of the frame, surrounded by completed data-center buildings. No completed building exists at the point to place coordinates on, so this is planned/under-construction. Coords left null.

QTS RIC3 DC5, 3543 Portugee Road, Sandston VA, part of the QTS Richmond 3 campus (five 'Freedom Design' buildings, DC1-DC5 at 3525-3543 Portugee Rd).
